content.claim: Poland's 1993 decentralization transferred school management to municipalities (gminas), creating fiscal exposure that made small rural schools vulnerable to local closure decisions rather than national policy direction.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Based on institutional history rather than quantitative outcome studies
Does not directly measure fiscal stress on gminas relative to school operating costs
Assumes causal link between decentralization and closure vulnerability without controlling for counterfactual centralized funding scenarios
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
